metric_value: Hercules should implement SAP R3 on HP 9000 Series 800 as sole supplier
notes: Based on five criteria analysis; HP recommended for all multiuser systems anticipated in 1993-1994

metric_value: Implement three-tier-plus topology instead of planned two-tier; two-tier has 'fatal flaws' for distributed enterprises
notes: Two-tier flaws: no consolidated executive data integrity + no backup/recovery/security services

metric_value: Decision will be winner-take-all; Hercules committed to one hardware supplier for next 3-5 years if selection made June 1993
notes: Based on evidence that enterprises cannot practically mix-and-match Unix variants in production

metric_value: 45%+ market share in commercial RISC/Unix systems
notes: HP market leader; 1993 growth rate for HP 9000 claimed to exceed 40%

metric_value: 15% market share in commercial RISC/Unix systems
notes: Majority of IBM 1992 sales to small-to-medium enterprises via VARs; limited large enterprise experience

metric_value: HP 9000 Series 800 was original targeted hardware platform for SAP R3 redevelopment
notes: Oracle uses HP 9000 as Unix reference port; confirms HP's central role in SAP R3 ecosystem

metric_value: Original target: all R3 modules by mid-1994; rewriting proprietary mainframe database to Unix required Oracle adoption
notes: SAP R3 required Oracle as database (ADABAS alternative for higher performance)

metric_value: Applications grow at 30-40% per year for first three years after initial SAP R3 installation
notes: Aberdeen's observed pattern from other enterprise SAP implementations; drives need for maximum growth path

metric_value: Still beta — not ready for production; ISVs and early adopters confirmed; no TPC-A results available
notes: DEC had re-entered RISC/Unix market for 5th time; earlier abandoned VAX/Ultrix + MIPS/Ultrix + VAX/Unix SVR4 + Intel/SCO

metric_value: Production yields mostly 133-200 MHz; unable to produce 200 MHz in quantities; first-generation chip with no proven next-gen
notes: Aberdeen concerned about DEC's ability to implement next-generation Alpha with advanced silicon techniques

metric_value: 550 applications stated by ISVs as planned ports as of March 1993; multiuser support not a priority without demand
notes: Aberdeen interviews with ISVs indicated that multiuser Alpha/OSF1 support was not a major priority

metric_value: Strongly recommends Hercules NOT select DEC as supplier of choice for SAP R3
notes: Confirmed by DEC acquisition by Compaq 1998 — platform instability proved prescient

metric_value: Not highly stable according to user reports; major reason few large enterprises deployed large RS/6000 counts; new Toronto AIX development center expected to produce more stable AIX/6000 before end 1993
notes: FileNet and Wang (IBM-dedicated VARs) added competitive products due to AIX instability

metric_value: End of RIOS processor visible; entire IBM product line (PC + RS/6000 + AS/400 + mainframes) migrating to PowerPC 600
notes: PowerPC 600 (RIOS 2) not yet in production for multiuser evaluation; implies major OS + compiler changes with AIX/6000

metric_value: RS/6000 is viable but relatively poor choice vs HP 9000 Series 800; multiple deficiencies outweigh advantages
notes: IBM ISV support rated adequate; supplier experience and Unix stability rated inferior to HP

metric_value: 7 generations of PA-RISC implementations; Hitachi developing high-end next-gen; Samsung developing low-end
notes: Drawback: emphasis on floating point over integer performance; but TPC-A results still highest in industry

metric_value: HP 890-400 has more throughput capability than Hercules' current IBM 3090-200 mainframe
notes: IBM refuses to run TPC-A on 3090 line; HP result validates migration business case for Hercules

metric_name: HP as SAP and Oracle reference platform
metric_value: HP is RISC/Unix reference port for both SAP and Oracle; Oracle's most important multiuser platform in revenue
notes: Most ISVs for multiuser Unix marketplace port to HP first; then IBM RS/6000

metric_name: HP evaluation criterion 1 — Breadth of product line
metric_value: Only supplier with both high-end and midrange SMP; broadest product line; granular midrange by processor speed
notes: Critical because neither SAP nor Hercules could accurately size required platforms

metric_value: Longest history of Unix commitment; HP PSO claimed world's largest Unix services organization
notes: PSO claim not disputed by Aberdeen though quality could be greater

metric_name: HP evaluation criterion 3 — Stable Unix OS
metric_value: HP-UX in top tier for Unix stability; OpenView extensions shared with industry accelerated stable Unix acceptance
notes: No Unix OS yet matches proprietary OS stability; HP-UX is top tier among Unix variants

metric_name: HP evaluation criterion 4 — Upgradeable RISC processor
metric_value: 7 generations of PA-RISC; proven track record; Hitachi and Samsung developing next-gen implementations
notes: HP 890-400 highest TPC-A results despite floating-point optimization bias

metric_value: SAP and Oracle reference port; majority of ISVs for multiuser Unix marketplace
notes: IBM RS/6000 is second; ports arrive on HP first then IBM

metric_value: Hercules goal to remove mainframe by Q1 1996; achievable with HP 9000 Series 800 and SAP R3
notes: Contingent on implementing third-tier topology and HP as sole supplier

metric_value: HP dominated commercial RISC/Unix through late 1990s; acquired Compaq (including DEC/Tandem) in 2002 for $25B
notes: Aberdeen's market leadership assessment of HP validated; HP became dominant enterprise Unix vendor

metric_value: Acquired by Compaq for $9.6B in January 1998; Alpha platform discontinued; confirms Aberdeen's 1993 concerns
notes: Aberdeen's strong recommendation against DEC proved prescient; platform instability and Unix credibility gaps materialized

metric_value: Acquired by Ashland Inc. for $2.6B (total transaction $3.3B including debt) in 2008; creating major global specialty chemicals company
notes: SAP R3 implementation recommended in 1993 would have been in production well before 2008 acquisition

metric_value: SAP R3 became dominant ERP platform worldwide; SAP became largest enterprise software company globally
notes: Aberdeen's early endorsement of SAP R3 at Hercules aligned with what became industry-transforming software
